evals.name: new-package-bootstrap
evals.prompt: I want to create a new R package called 'datacleanr' for cleaning messy datasets. Walk me through the usethis workflow to create the package, set up Git, add an MIT license, create my first R file for a function called clean_names(), and set up testthat. Give me the exact usethis function calls in order.

assertions.description: Calls are in correct order (create -> git -> license -> R file -> testthat -> test)

evals.name: add-dependency-and-docs
evals.prompt: I have an R package that needs to use dplyr for data manipulation and stringr for string operations. I also want to set up a README.Rmd, add pkgdown for a website, and create a vignette called 'intro'. What usethis functions should I call? Also show me how to properly reference dplyr functions in my R code.

evals.name: github-ci-release
evals.prompt: My R package is ready for its first CRAN submission. I need to: set up GitHub Actions for R CMD check, add CRAN submission comments, create a release issue checklist, add a lifecycle badge, and prepare a NEWS.md file. Show me the usethis calls. Also, how do I check my Git/GitHub setup is correct?

assertions.description: Uses use_github_action() for R CMD check
Correct function choices for CRAN release prep

evals.name: pr-workflow-contributor
evals.prompt: I want to contribute to the r-lib/gargle package on GitHub. I need to fork it, clone it locally, create a branch called 'fix-token-refresh', make my changes, and submit a pull request. Show me the complete usethis pr_* workflow from start to finish.

assertions.description: Correct PR workflow order (fork/clone -> init -> work -> push -> finish)
